task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows the Moodle course page for 'Introduction to Biology' or 'BIO101'.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Gradebook Export page
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows the Gradebook page, specifically navigating to the 'Export' tab or selecting 'Export' from a dropdown menu.
The 'Plain text file' or 'CSV' option is selected in the export format menu or tab.

task_completion.description: Enable the inclusion of feedback comments
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the export settings page, the checkbox for 'Include feedback in export' (or similar wording for feedback comments) is checked.

task_completion.description: Save the exported file to the correct path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager window shows 'BIO101_Grades.csv' inside the 'Documents' directory, a browser 'Save As' dialog shows the file being saved to '/home/ga/Documents/BIO101_Grades.csv', or a terminal shows the downloaded file being moved/renamed to this exact path.

integrity.description: The agent interacts with the actual Moodle web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses a web browser to navigate Moodle, rather than just typing the final file content into a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent does not manually create the CSV file from scratch
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent downloads the file from Moodle. It does not open a text editor or use terminal echo commands to manually type out the CSV contents.

integrity.description: The file is downloaded via the browser's export functionality
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the download/export button in the Moodle GUI rather than using curl/wget or a pre-made script to fetch the file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target course is 'Introduction to Biology' with shortname 'BIO101'. The expected filename is 'BIO101_Grades.csv' and it must be saved to the exact path '/home/ga/Documents/BIO101_Grades.csv'. The export must include feedback comments.
